name: Cous Cous with Lamb
recipeIngredient: 1 Piece of Fresh Ginger, cut into the same size as the garlic
1.5 pounds White Turnips, peeled and cut into 1-inch cubes or chunks
1 small Butternut Squash, peeled, seeded, and cut into 1-inch chunks
1 small Egg Plant, cut into 1-inch chunks
2 Carrots, peeled and cut into 1-inch chunks
1 Ripe Tomato, cut into half, remove seeds, and cut into 1/4 slices
1 medium Zucchini, washed and cut into 1-inch chunks
recipeInstructions:
Cous Cous:
1. Quarter teaspoon Pepper
2. Quarter teaspoon Salt
3. Third Stick of Unsalted Butter
4. 2 cups Instant Cous Cous
5. 4 ounces Dried Figs, cut into 1/4 pieces
1. Bring 1-3/4 cups water to a boil in a large saucepan.
2. Add pepper and salt, melted butter, and cous cous. Stir to coat well.
3. Stir in figs and add seasoned water. Stir well.
4. Cover and let stand for 10 minutes.
5. Divide the cous cous into four individual dishes.
6. Make a well in the center of cous cous and fill with meat and vegetables.
Lamb:
1. Cover lamb with cold water and bring to a boil, drain in colander and rinse with cold water.
2. Put lamb in a large Dutch oven and add 3 cups of water.
3. Puree garlic and ginger together in a blender to make a paste.
4. Add the puree to lamb along with cumin, salt, and tomato paste.
5. Bring to a boil and simmer gently covered for 45 minutes.
6. Add all the vegetables except the last three ingredients.
7. Bring to a boil and simmer for 15 minutes.
8. Add the last three ingredients and liquid.
9. Bring to a boil for 15 more minutes (gently).
